summary: Deep search: Google brand AI tools (Pomelli/Gemini), AI methods to read client data, AI for strategy, free wireable data tools — synthesized into what OGZ should wire for its 3 pilots

findings.name: Pomelli (Google Labs + DeepMind) — "Business DNA"
findings.what_it_does: Experimental AI marketing tool, launched Oct 28 2025 (blog.google/innovation-and-ai/.../pomelli/). 3-step flow that is almost EXACTLY OGZ's architecture: (1) you give it your website URL, it auto-extracts a 'Business DNA' profile = tone of voice, custom fonts, color palette, images, visual style; (2) it suggests tailored campaign IDEAS from that DNA; (3) it generates on-brand assets for social/web/ads, editable text+image, then download. This is the single closest existing product to what OGZ is building. Its lesson for OGZ: the 'read the brand first, then generate on-brand' (Business DNA → ideas → assets) loop is the right spine — OGZ's per-client organs ARE a hand-built, deeper Business DNA.
findings.access: FREE public beta (Google says hundreds of images/videos free during beta). Available in Saudi Arabia as a COUNTRY (support.google.com/labs/answer/16945066 lists KSA, UAE, Egypt, Qatar, Kuwait, Bahrain, Oman, Jordan, etc.). BUT English-only — Arabic is NOT a supported language. NO API, NO Saudi/Arabic — it is a web app only. Not in the EU.
findings.use_for_ogz: DO NOT wire it (no API, English-only). USE IT AS A SPEC + competitor benchmark: sign up from KSA (works in English), run albaik.com / a jirish site / myfitness.sa through it, screenshot its Business DNA output and campaign ideas — that is a free, Google-built reference for what a brand-extraction + idea layer should produce, and proof OGZ's Arabic-native version is a real gap Google has NOT filled for Saudi. Mohamed can rate Google's English ideas vs OGZ's Arabic ones (taste test).

findings.name: Gemini API / Google AI Studio (the actual engine, not a 'marketing tool')
findings.what_it_does: Google has no Arabic brand-content API product — but the raw Gemini API is the wireable piece. It does strong Arabic generation, structured/JSON output (schema-constrained — good for forcing OGZ's caption+strategy fields), long context (1M) to hold a whole client corpus, and multimodal (can read the brand's images/screenshots). This is how you'd actually replicate Pomelli's 'Business DNA' for Arabic: feed the client's site+posts+reviews, ask Gemini for a structured brand profile, then generate captions against it.
findings.access: PAID API, available in Saudi Arabia (confirmed on ai.google.dev/gemini-api/docs/available-regions — KSA, UAE, Egypt all listed). Flash-Lite ~$0.10/1M input, Flash ~$1.50/$9 per 1M in/out (mid-2026). Free tier exists in AI Studio but our GOOGLE_AI_STUDIO_KEY free quota is EXHAUSTED (per MEMORY.md) — would need billing enabled on the GCP project. Gemini Personal Intelligence launched across the Arab world Apr 14 2026 with Arabic+English; Google is building an Arabic-model AI hub in Dammam with PIF — so Arabic quality is a Google priority.
findings.use_for_ogz: This is the ONLY Google piece OGZ can actually wire. Two honest caveats: (1) money — OpenAI is the funded key right now, Gemini billing is NOT enabled, so do NOT switch the brain to Gemini just for this; (2) the durable asset is the PROMPT (per money-discipline rule), not the model. Recommendation: borrow the Pomelli STRUCTURE (URL/corpus → structured Business-DNA JSON → ideas → captions) and implement it with the already-funded OpenAI key; keep Gemini as a cheap second opinion only if billing is later turned on. The structure is the value, not Google's brand.

findings.name: Google Ads AI — AI Max / Asset Studio / 'Ask Advisor' (Gemini-in-Ads, GML 2026)
findings.what_it_does: At Google Marketing Live 2026 Google rebuilt Ads around Gemini: 'AI Brief' lets you describe your brand in natural language (what to say, what to avoid, tone, audiences) and it generates ad copy; 'Asset Studio' uses Gemini (incl. video) to turn brand guidelines → image/video ad assets via natural-language prompts; 'Ask Advisor' is a Gemini agent across Ads/Analytics/Merchant Center as a strategy partner. Conceptually overlaps OGZ (brand-brief → on-brand assets).
findings.access: Locked INSIDE Google Ads — you must run/pay for ad campaigns; there is NO standalone 'generate me an Instagram caption' product, and the asset gen serves Google ad placements, not Instagram. Google Ads API exists but is for campaign management, not a content-generation endpoint. Arabic asset generation quality for Saudi is UNVERIFIED in sources. Effectively not usable as a content engine for OGZ's Instagram use-case.
findings.use_for_ogz: NOT usable for OGZ's pilots (it's an ad-buying tool, content is a byproduct tied to Google ad inventory, not IG). One transferable idea only: the 'AI Brief — describe brand in natural language incl. what to AVOID' pattern is good UX; OGZ's strategy stage should explicitly capture per-client 'what to avoid' (mirrors OGZ's Saudi red-lines / kill_patterns). Ignore the product, steal the 'negative brief' idea.

findings.name: Google Product Studio (Merchant Center) — AI product imagery
findings.what_it_does: Free AI IMAGE tool in Merchant Center: background removal/clean white bg, image enhancement/upscaling, custom 'Generate Scene' backgrounds for seasonal/campaign visuals, and static-photo → short video. 'Generated for You' auto-proposes enhanced product shots from your feed. It is IMAGE-only — it does not write captions or strategy.
findings.access: FREE. Available to all Merchant Center users except Russia, so accessible from Saudi. BUT: works best in Dutch/English/French/German/etc. — Arabic is NOT in its supported-language list, and it generates imagery not Arabic copy so language matters less. The VIDEO feature is gated to AU/CA/IN/JP/UK/US only — NOT Saudi. Requires a Merchant Center account + a product feed (built for e-commerce/Shopping, awkward for a gym or cloud-kitchen).
findings.use_for_ogz: Marginal. OGZ already has its own image stack (OpenClaw v3.7 + fal.ai, 94-chain prompt canon) and is money-disciplined on fal schnell ~$0.003/img — Product Studio adds nothing there and needs a Shopping feed OGZ's pilots don't have. Possible niche: free background-clean/upscale for a real product photo (e.g. an albaik meal, a jirish dish) before it goes into a post — but not worth wiring an account for 3 pilots. Skip for now; note it as a free fallback for product-shot cleanup if a client supplies raw photos.

research.conclusion: Q1 bottom line for OGZ: Google has built the EXACT product OGZ is building — Pomelli's 'Business DNA → campaign ideas → on-brand assets' is OGZ's spine, confirmed by Google's own launch (blog.google, Oct 28 2025). But Google has NOT shipped it for OGZ's market: Pomelli is English-ONLY (available in KSA the country, but no Arabic), web-only, no API. That is OGZ's actual moat — Arabic-native, Saudi-taste, autonomous Instagram is a real gap Google has not filled. The ONLY Google piece OGZ can wire today is the raw Gemini API (available in Saudi per ai.google.dev/available-regions, strong Arabic, structured JSON output, 1M context to hold a client corpus) — but with two honest flags: (1) MONEY — OpenAI is the funded key, Gemini free quota is exhausted and billing isn't enabled, so don't switch engines just for this; (2) the durable asset is the PROMPT/architecture, not Google's brand. Concrete recommendation: copy Pomelli's STRUCTURE (corpus/URL → structured 'Business DNA' profile → ideas → captions) and the Ads 'negative brief / what-to-avoid' pattern, and implement them on the already-funded OpenAI key inside OGZ's research-before-strategy stage. Product Studio (free, image-only, no Arabic copy, video not in Saudi) and Google Ads AI (locked inside paid ad campaigns, no standalone content API) are NOT usable for the 3 IG pilots. Sources: blog.google/innovation-and-ai/models-and-research/google-labs/pomelli/ (Oct 28 2025); support.google.com/labs/answer/16945066 (KSA listed, English-only); ai.google.dev/gemini-api/docs/available-regions (KSA included); blog.google/intl/en-mena/.../free-gemini-pro-students-saudi-arabia-egypt/ (Gemini Arabic+English in KSA); blog.google/products/ads-commerce/google-marketing-live-2026-collection/ + asset-studio-updates (GML 2026 Gemini Ads); support.google.com/merchants/answer/13708167 + answer/15717899 (Product Studio, video gated to 6 non-Saudi countries).

findings.name: Lucidya (Arabic-native social listening + CX intelligence)
findings.what_it_does: Jeddah, Saudi-Arabia-based AI platform built specifically for Arabic NLP/dialect. Monitors brand + competitor + topic conversations across X, Instagram, Facebook, WhatsApp, YouTube comments, live chat and email; real-time sentiment, share-of-voice, trend detection, audience demographics. Frost & Sullivan named it #1 in MENA on both Growth and Innovation in its Dec 2025 Radar for AI-Powered CX/Media Intelligence (globenewswire.com/.../3207409, 2025-12-18). Strongest of the listening tools at actual Saudi dialect, the exact gap OGZ has.
findings.access: PAID, demo/sales-quote model (no transparent public price; G2/Capterra/TrustRadius list it but no figure). Free trial, no card required. A public REST Social Listening API exists: docs.lucidya.com/docs/Social-Listening-api/rqwky70duwx76-get-started (verified live, JS-rendered). Saudi-based, Arabic-first — the only listening vendor where Arabic/Saudi dialect is the core product, not an add-on. URL: lucidya.com/en/products/social-listening
findings.use_for_ogz: The premium option for the 'READ the market' stage and the single best Arabic-dialect fit. Its API could feed competitor share-of-voice + Saudi-dialect sentiment directly into a per-client research organ. But it is sales-quote paid — against money-discipline for a 3-pilot test. Recommend: take the free trial to pull a one-time competitor/sentiment snapshot for jurisha/albaik/myfitness, NOT a wired dependency yet. Albaik (big QSR) will have real volume; myfitness (Tabuk gym) likely too thin for listening to return much.

findings.name: Brand24 (affordable social listening w/ Arabic support + API)
findings.what_it_does: Social listening across Instagram, X, FB, TikTok, news, blogs, reviews. AI sentiment, hashtag/mention tracking, anomaly alerts, influencer scoring. Explicitly supports Arabic language and is repeatedly cited as a top pick for Saudi SMEs (brand24.com/blog/instagram-social-listening, 2026; arbaaa.com Saudi guide).
findings.access: PAID but cheap vs enterprise: Individual ~$49-149/mo, Team ~$119-199/mo (Capterra 2026). API is an add-on at ~$99 (oreateai.com Brand24 API breakdown). Arabic supported. Available in Saudi. URL: brand24.com
findings.use_for_ogz: The realistic paid listening tool for thin-data SMEs: one ~$49-149/mo seat could cover all 3 pilots' brand+competitor monitoring, and the +$99 API makes it wireable into OGZ's research stage. Arabic support is real but weaker than Lucidya at Saudi dialect — eyeball sentiment output before trusting it (Rule #9). Good cost/coverage middle ground.

findings.name: Talkwalker / Brandwatch / Sprout Social (enterprise listening — reference only)
findings.what_it_does: Full enterprise social-listening + consumer-intelligence suites (image recognition, virality prediction, large historical archives, competitor benchmarking). Strong English/global, weaker dedicated Saudi-dialect than Lucidya.
findings.access: PAID, enterprise. Talkwalker ~$9.6k-$27k/yr, no public price (prowly.com 2025). Brandwatch ~$800-$5,000/mo, demo-gated (trustradius 2026). Sprout Social $79-$399/user/mo (sproutsocial.com/pricing). All available in Saudi via resellers.
findings.use_for_ogz: OUT OF SCOPE for a money-disciplined 3-pilot test — overkill and 10-50x the cost of Brand24 for SMEs with thin data. Listed so the decision is honest: do NOT wire these now. Reconsider only if OGZ lands an enterprise Saudi brand (ROSHN/Almarai-tier) where the historical archive + competitor benchmarking pays off.

findings.name: Apify actors (Instagram + Google Maps reviews scraping) — the free/cheap data layer
findings.what_it_does: Pay-per-result cloud scrapers. Instagram Profile/Post/Reel scrapers pull posts, captions, likes, comments, hashtags, follower counts; Google Maps Reviews scrapers pull star ratings, review text and owner responses for any location (a brand's own + competitors').
findings.access: CHEAP, scriptable API. IG Profile Scraper $2.30-2.60/1K results; IG Post $1.00/1K; alternatives down to $0.40/1K (apify.com/apify/instagram-profile-scraper). Google Maps reviews ~$0.05-$3.00/1K — ~97% cheaper than Google Places API ($170/10K). $5 free credit. OGZ ALREADY HAS an Apify Starter token with credits (per MEMORY.md). Available globally incl. Saudi. URL: apify.com
findings.use_for_ogz: The highest-ROI wire right now and already paid-for. Build the research-before-strategy stage on this: (1) IG scraper → pull each pilot's own posts + 3-5 Saudi competitors → engagement-winner analysis (which captions/formats/post-times win); (2) Google Maps Reviews scraper → albaik + jurisha branch reviews + competitors → Saudi-dialect complaint/praise themes feeding strategy. This directly fattens the thin organs (myfitness's 6 moments) with real market data. Note: IG audience demographics are NOT scrapable — only public post/engagement data.

findings.name: Instagram Graph API (Insights) — first-party engagement + audience data
findings.what_it_does: Meta's official API. /media/insights gives per-post reach, impressions, saves, video views, likes, comments; /user/insights gives account reach, profile activity, follower growth, and demographic audience breakdown (top segments by city/age/gender). The ground-truth 'what actually won' + 'who the audience really is' for an OWNED account.
findings.access: FREE from Meta. Requires the client's IG to be a Business/Creator account and OAuth permissions (instagram_basic, instagram_manage_insights, pages_read_engagement) — i.e. the CLIENT must grant access; this is a mohamed_must / client-consent gate, not self-serve. 2025 caveats (developers.facebook.com/docs/instagram-platform/insights): follower demographics hidden under 100 followers; v21 deprecated profile_views, website_clicks, non-Reel video_views (Jan 8 2025); only top 45 audience segments returned.
findings.use_for_ogz: The single most accurate engagement+audience source — but only for accounts OGZ is granted access to. For albaik/jurisha/myfitness this is the correct 'engagement-winner' + 'real audience' input IF the client connects. Stage a client-consent card (OGZ flow: EXTRACT needs this token). Until granted, fall back to Apify public-post scraping. Free, so no money concern — the blocker is consent + Business-account setup, not cost.

findings.name: Arabic embeddings + RAG stack (open-source, for a per-client brand corpus)
findings.what_it_does: The 'brains read the data themselves' layer. Embed each client's posts + reviews + competitor corpus into a vector store and retrieve for grounded strategy/caption generation. Best Arabic embedder: Omartificial-Intelligence-Space/Arabic-Triplet-Matryoshka-V2 (top Arabic STS, avg 74.5, fine-tuned from AraBERTv02, HF). Orchestrate with RAGFlow (infiniflow/ragflow) for ingest/chunk/embed/retrieve+rerank.
findings.access: FREE / open-source (Apache/MIT). Runs locally — no per-call cost, fits money-discipline and the Anthropic-dry situation. HF: huggingface.co/Omartificial-Intelligence-Space/Arabic-Triplet-Matryoshka-V2 ; HF guide 'Building a Custom Arabic Semantic Search Model ... for RAG'; github.com/infiniflow/ragflow. Arabic-native by design.
findings.use_for_ogz: Replaces/augments OpenAI embeddings for the per-client memory OGZ already wants. A local Arabic embedder means the per-client RAG corpus (organs + scraped posts + reviews) costs $0/query and keeps Saudi-dialect semantics. Recommend wiring Arabic-Matryoshka-V2 as the embedding model behind the client memory, with RAGFlow only if a heavier pipeline is needed — for 3 thin pilots a simple FAISS/Chroma index is enough; don't over-build.

findings.name: Arabic sentiment / dialect NLP repos + Saudi datasets (open-source)
findings.what_it_does: Turn scraped Saudi reviews/comments into structured INSIGHT (sentiment, complaint topics, dialect). AraBERT (aub-mind/arabert, 77GB pretrain) + CAMeLBERT-DA (dialectal Arabic). Saudi-specific labeled data: nora-twairesh/AraSenti (Saudi-dialect tweets, 4-label) and the HARD hotel-reviews / 'Arabic 100k Reviews' / Omcca (Saudi+Jordanian) sets via 4mekki4/arabic-nlp-da. Curated index: h9-tec/Awesome_Arabic_NLP.
findings.access: FREE / open-source on GitHub + HF. Runs locally, no API cost. Genuinely Saudi-dialect-aware (AraSenti is Saudi-tweet-trained) — a real edge over generic English sentiment that mislabels Gulf Arabic.
findings.use_for_ogz: Wire CAMeLBERT-DA or a fine-tuned AraBERT as the sentiment/topic classifier over Apify-scraped reviews + comments, calibrated on AraSenti. This is how reviews → 'top 3 things Saudis praise/complain about per brand' becomes a strategy input. Caution (Rule #9): validate on a held-out Saudi sample and eyeball before any number reaches Mohamed — Gulf dialect + sarcasm break generic sentiment.

findings.name: Engagement-winner analysis pattern (DIY over scraped data) + reference repos
findings.what_it_does: The method, not a product: rank a brand's own posts by engagement rate, then correlate winners against format (Reel/carousel/static), caption length, hashtags, topic, and post-time to extract 'what wins for THIS brand.' 2025 benchmarks to grade against: IG ~0.45-0.6% avg engagement; Reels+carousels+authentic UGC beat polished statics; lower-frequency-higher-quality wins the AI-curated feed (adobe.com/express engagement guide; digitalinformationworld.com 2025). Starter code: gabrielamgelo/Instagram-Posts-Analysis (Instaloader scrape → EDA → Streamlit viz).
findings.access: FREE — pure analysis over data you already scraped (Apify/IG API). Instaloader (github.com/topics/instaloader) is free but login-throttled, so prefer Apify for reliability. Benchmarks are public articles.
findings.use_for_ogz: This IS the 'engagement-winner analysis' organ. For each pilot: scrape own posts → compute per-post engagement rate → surface the top-decile winners and their shared traits → feed those traits as constraints into the caption/strategy brain. Critically it lets a strategy be DATA-derived ('your carousels outperform statics 3:1') not template — answering the gap myfitness's 6 moments leave. Pure compute, $0, wireable today.

findings.name: SparkToro (audience research / where-they-hang-out)
findings.what_it_does: Audience intelligence: describe or upload an audience and it returns the channels, publications, podcasts, hashtags, accounts and topics that audience actually engages with, plus demographics and persona segmentation. May 2025 added an AI 'Describe Your Audience' natural-language tool and email-list upload→segment.
findings.access: FREEMIUM: free plan = 5 searches/month (enough for 3 pilots' initial passes); AI features gated to agency/enterprise paid tiers. URL: sparktoro.com (coywolf.com 2025 update). Coverage is global/English-skewed — Saudi/Arabic audience depth is WEAK; built mostly on Western web/social signals.
findings.use_for_ogz: Useful for a quick free directional read on each pilot's audience interests/adjacent topics, but HONEST FLAG: SparkToro is thin on Saudi-specific audience data, so treat output as a hypothesis, not Saudi ground truth. For real Saudi audience insight, the IG Graph API demographics (owned account) + scraped competitor-follower overlap beat it. Use the free tier opportunistically; do not pay for it for these pilots.

research.conclusion: Q2 answer for OGZ: a money-disciplined research-before-strategy stage for the 3 thin-data Saudi pilots should be built on a FREE/CHEAP open stack OGZ mostly already owns, not enterprise listening suites. RECOMMENDED WIRE (today, ~$0): (1) Apify (already a paid Starter token) to scrape each pilot's own IG posts + 3-5 Saudi competitors + Google Maps reviews — this is the cheapest, most direct way to fatten thin organs like myfitness's 6 moments with real market data; (2) an engagement-winner analysis pass over that scraped data (pure compute) to derive data-grounded 'what wins for THIS brand' instead of a generic template; (3) a local Arabic NLP layer — Arabic-Triplet-Matryoshka-V2 embeddings for the per-client RAG memory + CAMeLBERT-DA/AraBERT (calibrated on the Saudi-dialect AraSenti dataset) for review/comment sentiment — all open-source, $0/query, and Saudi-dialect-aware, which matters because generic English sentiment mislabels Gulf Arabic. CLIENT-CONSENT GATE (free but needs the client): Instagram Graph API Insights is the ground-truth engagement + real-audience source for any account OGZ is granted — stage a consent card per pilot. PAID, OPTIONAL: Brand24 (~$49-149/mo + $99 API) is the realistic Arabic listening add-on if scraping proves insufficient; Lucidya is the best Saudi-dialect fit but sales-quote-paid — take its free trial for a one-time competitor/sentiment snapshot rather than wiring it. HONEST FLAGS: Talkwalker/Brandwatch/Sprout are enterprise overkill ($800-$27k) — do not buy for 3 pilots; SparkToro is thin on Saudi/Arabic audience data — use only the free tier as a hypothesis; IG audience demographics are NOT scrapable (Graph API only, with consent); and per Rule #9, every Arabic-sentiment/engagement number must be eyeballed on a real Saudi sample before it reaches Mohamed because dialect and sarcasm break off-the-shelf models. NOTE: the GitHub MCP returned 'Bad credentials' so repo findings were verified via web search against real github.com URLs rather than the MCP.

findings.name: CrewAI marketing_strategy crew (official example) — the canonical research→strategy agent chain
findings.what_it_does: Open-source reference implementation of a 4-agent marketing pipeline that mirrors exactly what OGZ needs: Lead Market Analyst → Chief Marketing Strategist → Creative Content Creator → Chief Creative Director (reviewer). It runs 5 sequential tasks: (1) Research task — industry + competitive landscape + engagement patterns; (2) Project Understanding — audience segmentation; (3) Marketing Strategy task — SYNTHESIZES research into a strategy doc with objectives, demographic focus, core messaging, channels, and measurable KPIs; (4) Campaign Ideas — 5 distinct concepts; (5) Copy Creation. The strategy is NOT improvised — it's a structured task whose expected_output schema forces objectives/messaging/tactics/metrics. Verified the actual agents.yaml + tasks.yaml. Uses GPT-4o by default, Serper for search.
findings.access: Free, open-source (MIT). Repo: https://github.com/crewAIInc/crewAI-examples/tree/main/crews/marketing_strategy (verified live, June 2026). No Saudi/Arabic restriction — runs locally on OGZ's OpenAI key (funded). Python/CrewAI. The structure is portable to any LLM.
findings.use_for_ogz: This is the closest public mirror of OGZ's own RABIE→Strategist→CD chain — use it as a STRUCTURE CHECK, not a copy. The load-bearing lesson: strategy is a typed task with a forced output schema (objectives, audience, messaging pillars, channels, KPIs), with research as a HARD upstream dependency. OGZ's research-before-strategy stage should emit exactly this schema per client (jurisha/albaik/myfitness) so the renderer few-shots from a real strategy, not a vibe. Cheap to run on funded OpenAI key.

findings.name: yashmishra20/Automating-a-Marketing-Team-with-agents — Instagram-specific CrewAI strategy crew
findings.what_it_does: A CrewAI build aimed specifically at Instagram content (closest to OGZ's exact job). 4 agents: Market Researcher (competitive intel via Serper) → Content Strategist (plans the content CALENDAR + pillars) → Visual Creator (writes visual/image descriptions) → Copywriter (captions). Output = research report + content calendar + visual asset descriptions + copy variations. Shows the wiring of research → calendar → per-post brief → caption, which is the OGZ pipeline minus the Saudi/Arabic layer.
findings.access: Free, open-source. Repo: https://github.com/yashmishra20/Automating-a-Marketing-Team-with-agents (verified, small repo ~4 commits). GPT-4o/3.5 + Serper API (Serper has a free tier). Runs locally; no regional block.
findings.use_for_ogz: Concrete proof the Instagram research→calendar→caption flow works as 4 typed agents. The 'Content Strategist builds the calendar' step is the missing-middle for OGZ's thin clients: instead of writing 20 random posts, the strategist allocates pillars across the month FIRST. Borrow the calendar-allocation task; replace its generic strategist prompt with OGZ's Arabic + Saudi red-line + per-client-organ context.

findings.name: StoryChief 9-step AI Content Strategy framework — the anti-generic checklist
findings.what_it_does: A named, productized 9-stage framework for turning brand inputs into a converting content strategy: (1) Website/asset analysis → gaps; (2) Company description (who you help, problem, differentiation); (3) Brand Voice profile (tone/sentence/format); (4) Content Pillars (3-5 themes to OWN); (5) Audience Intent (what people MEAN, not keywords); (6) Content Ideas backlog (quick wins / authority / conversion mix); (7) Briefs (intent + outline + CTA); (8) Create/Distribute/Measure; (9) Monthly improvement loop. Its explicit thesis: generic AI is avoided by anchoring every draft to business context + audience reality + brand voice + performance feedback.
findings.access: Framework is free to read/copy (it's a methodology, not a gated tool): https://storychief.io/blog/ai-content-strategy. The StoryChief product is paid SaaS; the FRAMEWORK costs nothing to implement. No Saudi/Arabic restriction on the method.
findings.use_for_ogz: Use steps 2-4-5-7 as the REQUIRED FIELDS of OGZ's per-client strategy object: company-description, brand-voice profile, 3-5 content pillars, audience-intent map, per-post brief. These four fields are exactly what distinguishes a real strategy from an LLM template — and they're what OGZ's thin clients (myfitness = 6 moments) most lack, so the research stage must FILL them from market data, not leave them blank. This directly maps to Rule #11 (diagnose gaps → ask only the gap-questions).

findings.name: The 5-Pillar Framework for trustworthy AI content (Greg Jarboe, Search Engine Journal)
findings.what_it_does: An editorial framework (not a tool) naming the 5 things that make AI content credible vs. detectable-slop: (1) Strategy First, Automation Second — architectural brief BEFORE generation; (2) Visceral Storytelling — emotional resonance over facts; (3) Multimodal Optimization — adapt to each platform's native dialect, don't syndicate identical assets; (4) Measure What Matters — watch-time/scroll-depth not likes; (5) Ethics + human-oversight checkpoint before publish. Core line: 'A vague brief produces generic fluff. A structured brief with clear context, defined constraints, and specific tone guidelines produces something you can actually work with.'
findings.access: Free article, April 4 2026: https://www.searchenginejournal.com/the-5-pillar-framework-for-ai-content-that-audiences-actually-trust/568860/. No regional restriction.
findings.use_for_ogz: This is the philosophical backbone for why OGZ's whole architecture (structured brief → caption, human/Mohamed judge gate) is RIGHT, expressed as an external authority. Pillar 1 = OGZ's structured brief; Pillar 5 = Mohamed's click-gate. The gap it exposes: OGZ currently measures captions by Mohamed's taste-vote (good) but has no behavioral metric (Pillar 4) once posts ship — the research stage should set a per-client success metric (saves/shares/reach) so the monthly loop has a number, not just taste.

findings.name: Classic strategy frameworks AI agents wrap (STP, JTBD, AARRR, content pillars) — the scaffolds, not tools
findings.what_it_does: The frameworks the good agent systems force the LLM through instead of free-prompting: STP (Segment→Target→Position) for who/where to play; Jobs-to-be-Done (customers 'hire' a product for a job — moves past surface positioning to the real need); Content Pillars (3-5 themes to own); AARRR/pirate-funnel for measuring across the journey; North Star Metric. The pattern that beats a single prompt: role-specialized multi-agent (each agent thinks like one platform/function) and forcing the model to emit JTBD + STP + pillars as STRUCTURED FIELDS rather than prose.
findings.access: All free, public methodologies. Refs: https://strategicpete.com/blog/7-marketing-frameworks-for-this-2025/ and https://www.beehiiv.com/blog/marketing-frameworks-bf28. JTBD discourse: https://jobstobedone.substack.com. No regional restriction; framework-only, no API needed.
findings.use_for_ogz: The cheapest highest-leverage move: don't buy a strategy tool — make OGZ's strategy stage emit a fixed JSON schema {segment, target, positioning_statement, jobs_to_be_done[], content_pillars[3-5], north_star_metric, monthly_pillar_allocation}. For thin clients, JTBD is the unlock: myfitness's 6 moments can't give a calendar, but 'why does a Tabuk member hire a gym' (discipline, community, post-work-energy, Ramadan-cut) yields pillars market-research can fill. This is what makes the strategy SPECIFIC instead of a template.

findings.name: Arabic-first signal + MENA content-pillar mix (the localization that makes the strategy real for Saudi)
findings.what_it_does: Market research finding that the strategy layer must encode: Arabic-first posts get 15-25% HIGHER engagement in KSA vs English/translated content; a working MENA pillar mix skews ~40% educational (how-to/tips/insights) to build expertise, with Reels + Arabic captions + clear CTA as the Instagram default. This is the difference between a strategy that scores in Riyadh and a US-template strategy that doesn't.
findings.access: Free reports (June 2026): https://thehovi.com/blog/industry-guides/social-media-marketing-gcc-mena-guide-2026 and https://eclipseadagency.com/blog/insights-content-marketing-strategies-saudi-arabia-2025-2/. SDAIA-backed national AI push noted. KSA = fastest-growing MENA ad market (>18% YoY).
findings.use_for_ogz: Bake these as DEFAULTS/constraints in the strategy schema, not discoveries to re-derive per client: Arabic-first (already OGZ's core), pillar-mix prior (~40% educational), Reels+CTA format. The 15-25% Arabic-engagement number is a real, citable justification for OGZ's entire Arabic-native thesis — useful in the strategy doc shown to a client. NOTE: these are blog/agency figures, not a primary study — treat the 15-25% as directional, verify against a client's own IG insights before quoting as fact (Rule #9).

research.conclusion:
Q3 answer: AI turns research into a GOOD strategy by FORCING the LLM through a typed framework with a structured output schema, with research as a hard upstream dependency — not by free-prompting "write me a strategy." The verified pattern across every real system (CrewAI marketing_strategy crew, the Instagram CrewAI repo, StoryChief's 9 steps, the SEJ 5-pillar framework) is identical to OGZ's own RABIE→Strategist→CD chain: role-specialized agents (Analyst → Strategist → Creator → Critic), and a strategy object whose REQUIRED fields are {segment/target, positioning, jobs-to-be-done, 3-5 content pillars, north-star metric, monthly pillar allocation}.

What separates a real strategy from a generic LLM template, stated by the sources and directly applicable to OGZ:
1. ANCHORING — every output traces to company-description + audience-INTENT (what they MEAN/the job they hire for) + brand-voice profile + performance feedback. A blank-anchor prompt produces "generic fluff" (Jarboe); a structured brief with context+constraints+tone produces usable work.
2. JOBS-TO-BE-DONE is the unlock for OGZ's thin-data problem — myfitness's 6 moments can't fill a calendar, but "why does a Tabuk member hire a gym" yields pillars that market research CAN fill. JTBD is how the research stage compensates for thin organs.
3. PILLARS-THEN-CALENDAR, not random posts — the strategist allocates 3-5 pillars across the month BEFORE any caption; this is the missing middle in OGZ's current flow.
4. A BEHAVIORAL METRIC per client (saves/shares/scroll), not just taste — Pillar 4. OGZ has Mohamed's taste-gate but no shipped-post metric to close the monthly loop.
5. SAUDI/ARABIC defaults baked into the schema: Arabic-first (cited +15-25% engagement vs English in KSA), ~40% educational pillar mix, Reels+CTA. Treat the 15-25% as directional agency data, not a primary study.

Concrete OGZ move (cheap, on the funded OpenAI key, no new vendor): add a strategy-stage that emits a fixed per-client JSON schema with the fields above; clone the CrewAI marketing_strategy + yashmishra20 Instagram crews as STRUCTURE references only (the typed Research→Strategy→Calendar→Caption task chain), and replace their generic strategist prompts with OGZ's Arabic + Saudi-red-line + per-client-organ context.

Honesty flags: GitHub MCP returned 'Bad credentials' — all repos were verified via WebFetch on github.com/raw.githubusercontent.com instead (CrewAI agents.yaml/tasks.yaml read directly, live June 2026). No vaporware found in this Q3 set: all six findings are real, free methodologies/repos with no Saudi block — the only paid items are the optional SaaS wrappers (StoryChief product), and OGZ does not need them. The one number to NOT quote as fact without client-IG verification is the 15-25% Arabic-engagement figure (agency blog, not primary).

findings.name: Instagram Graph API — own-account Insights (the client's OWN linked account)
findings.what_it_does: Official Meta API. Once a client connects their IG Business/Creator account (linked to a Facebook Page) and authorizes the app, you pull their OWN media + per-post engagement (like_count, comments_count, caption, timestamp, media_type, permalink) AND account Insights (reach, impressions, profile views, follower growth, audience demographics — age/gender/city/country). It tells the brain WHICH of the client's real posts won, by what, and who the audience actually is.
findings.access: FREE (Meta charges $0 — no key fee, no per-call charge). Saudi/Arabic: fully available, no geo restriction; Arabic captions/comments returned as UTF-8. THE KEY UNLOCK: you can stay in Development mode INDEFINITELY and serve the 3 pilots by adding each client's IG account as a 'tester' in the Meta app dashboard — NO App Review, NO Business Verification needed. Rate limit ~200 calls/user/hour. Setup: Meta Developer account + a Facebook Page per client linked to their IG Business account + OAuth token (refresh ~60 days). Docs: https://developers.facebook.com/docs/instagram-platform/reference/instagram-media/ (verified 2026-06-14). Dev-mode-forever confirmation: https://developers.facebook.com/community/threads/301542731090636 and https://www.getphyllo.com/post/instagram-api-pricing-explained-iv
findings.use_for_ogz: THE single biggest free win for the thin-data pilots. Fixes the thin-organ problem directly (myfitness.sa's 6 moments) by replacing guesses with the brand's OWN winner-data + real audience. Wire FIRST: get each pilot to link their IG, add as tester, pull last ~90 days of posts ranked by engagement → feeds 'what wins for THIS brand' into the research-before-strategy stage. Free, no review, Saudi/Arabic native.

findings.name: Instagram Graph API — business_discovery (COMPETITOR public accounts)
findings.what_it_does: Same API, business_discovery edge: query=business_discovery.username(albaikfood).fields(followers_count,media_count,media{caption,like_count,comments_count,timestamp,media_type,permalink}). Returns a competitor's public profile + recent posts WITH per-post like/comment counts. How OGZ reads the market: pull jurish/QSR/gym rivals' top posts, see what formats/topics win in the sector. Confirmed fields include like_count + comments_count + caption per media object.
findings.access: FREE at platform level, BUT the catch: querying accounts you DON'T own requires Advanced Access = Meta App Review + Business Verification (screencast of how data is used). NOT instant, not guaranteed. Saudi/Arabic data itself is fine. Docs: https://www.keyapi.ai/blog/instagram-business-discovery-api/ , https://www.getphyllo.com/post/instagram-api-pricing-explained-iv (verified 2026-06-14)
findings.use_for_ogz: The IDEAL competitor signal but gated behind App Review — treat as a LATER unlock once the pilots prove value. For NOW, get the exact same competitor engagement data via Apify (no review) instead. Don't block the research stage waiting on Meta verification.

findings.name: Apify — Instagram scrapers (apify/instagram-scraper, apidojo pay-per-result actors)
findings.what_it_does: Cloud scrapers returning any PUBLIC IG profile/post/hashtag/place data — captions, like/comment counts, timestamps, hashtags — by URL or handle, with NO Meta app, NO review, NO token. Programmatically callable via Apify API/SDK from OGZ's pipeline. Immediate way to get COMPETITOR + sector + hashtag intelligence today, sidestepping the App Review wall; also good for harvesting a client's own back-catalogue before they connect the Graph API.
findings.access: PAID but cheap, and OGZ ALREADY HAS an Apify Starter account with credits (per MEMORY.md). Official apify/instagram-scraper = $1.50/1,000 posts; comments $2.30/1,000. Cheaper apidojo actors ~$0.50/1,000 posts, profile scraper $0.01/profile (first 40 free). Free plan = $5/mo credits (~3,300 posts). Saudi/Arabic: works fine, Arabic text intact. https://apify.com/apify/instagram-scraper , https://apify.com/apidojo/instagram-scraper , https://apify.com/pricing (verified 2026-06-14)
findings.use_for_ogz: The practical competitor/market-reading tool to wire NOW (no Meta review needed; account already funded). For each pilot, scrape 5-10 sector rivals + relevant hashtags, cap at 20-50 posts/competitor/round (money-discipline rule), rank by engagement → 'what wins in this Saudi sector'. Also use to backfill a client's own post history before their Graph API link is set up.

findings.name: Google Trends — Saudi/Arabic demand signal (geo='SA') via trendspyg / official alpha
findings.what_it_does: Free search-interest data: interest-over-time, rising/top related queries, interest-by-region (sub-region = Riyadh/Makkah/Tabuk). The cheapest 'what is Saudi searching for NOW' layer — seasonal spikes (Ramadan, back-to-school late-July pay-day surge, National Day), trending food/fitness terms in Arabic, regional skew. Peer-reviewed Saudi studies use it in BOTH English and Arabic (geo='SA'), so Saudi/Arabic support is real and validated.
findings.access: FREE. geo='SA' + Arabic query strings fully supported. Wire via: (1) FREE official Google Trends API (alpha) — apply at https://developers.google.com/search/apis/trends , endpoint https://trends.googleapis.com/v1alpha/trends:query , 5yr history, JSON/CSV, geo-restrictable, BUT alpha = rolling approval + quotas (announced 2025-07-24: https://developers.google.com/search/blog/2025/07/trends-api). (2) FREE lib trendspyg (MIT, v0.6.1 2026-06-08, 125 countries incl SA, RSS+CSV) — `pip install trendspyg[all]` — https://github.com/flack0x/trendspyg. (3) Apify 'Free Google Trends Scraper' (geo param, API-callable) but bills $5/1k despite the name.
findings.use_for_ogz: Wire SECOND (free demand layer for all 3 pilots). Use trendspyg today for instant geo='SA' Arabic-keyword pulls (jirish dishes, fried-chicken cravings, gym/fitness terms; regional filter for myfitness's Tabuk). Apply to the official alpha API in parallel as the durable path. Feeds 'what the market wants right now / seasonal timing' into strategy.

findings.name: pytrends (the old default) — AVOID as primary
findings.what_it_does: The long-time Python Google Trends wrapper. Still installs and runs simple geo='SA' queries, but was ARCHIVED by maintainers (read-only since ~2025; GeneralMills/pytrends shows archived). Breaking changes go unpatched; users hit frequent 429 rate-limit errors.
findings.access: Free but UNMAINTAINED / fragile. https://github.com/GeneralMills/pytrends (public, archived). Saudi/Arabic queries technically work but reliability is poor.
findings.use_for_ogz: DO NOT build the research stage on this dead dependency. Use trendspyg (drop-in, maintained) or the official alpha API instead. Flagged only so OGZ doesn't waste a session on a 429-prone archived library.

findings.name: Honest gap-flags (what is NOT free/instant or is overstated for Saudi)
findings.what_it_does: Reality-check on the rest: (a) 'Glimpse API' and 'ScrapingBee Google Trends' are reliable but PAID SaaS — unneeded when trendspyg/official-alpha are free. (b) TikTok/Snapchat (both huge in Saudi) have NO free engagement API like IG — Snapchat especially is a blind spot; only paid scrapers reach it. (c) IG business_discovery's competitor reach REQUIRES App Review — not a today tool. (d) The Apify 'free' Google Trends actor is NOT actually free ($5/1k). None are vaporware, but the 'free + instant + Saudi' intersection is narrower than the marketing implies.
findings.access: Mixed. Genuinely free-now-and-Saudi-ready: Google Trends (geo=SA) + the client's OWN IG account via Graph dev-mode. Everything that reads COMPETITORS at scale either costs Apify credits or needs Meta App Review. Snapchat/TikTok engagement = effectively out of reach free.
findings.use_for_ogz: Set expectations honestly: the research-before-strategy stage can be FREE for own-account + demand-trends TODAY; competitor/market reading costs small (already-available) Apify credits until App Review is worth doing. Don't promise free competitor intelligence, and flag Snapchat/TikTok as current blind spots if a pilot leans on them.

research.conclusion: For Q4, four tools are concretely wireable into OGZ's research-before-strategy stage, and the free/paid split is sharper than the marketing suggests. (1) THE BIGGEST FREE UNLOCK is the Instagram Graph API on each pilot's OWN account: by adding eatjurisha/albaik/myfitness.sa as 'testers' in a Meta app, OGZ stays in Development mode forever — no App Review, no Business Verification, $0 — and pulls their real per-post engagement (like_count, comments_count, caption) PLUS audience Insights (reach, follower growth, age/gender/city). That directly fixes the thin-organ problem (myfitness's 6 moments) with the brand's own winner-data and real audience. Verified today against Meta docs + the dev community thread. (2) Reading COMPETITORS/the market is the catch: business_discovery on accounts you don't own needs Advanced Access (App Review + Business Verification) — a later unlock, not a today tool. The immediate substitute is Apify's IG scrapers (~$0.50-1.50/1,000 posts), and OGZ ALREADY has an Apify Starter account with credits — so competitor/sector/hashtag pulls are cheap and need no Meta review. Respect money-discipline: cap at 20-50 posts/competitor/round. (3) Google Trends gives free Saudi demand signal (geo='SA', Arabic keywords, sub-region down to Riyadh/Tabuk), validated in peer-reviewed Saudi research — wire it via the maintained free library trendspyg (NOT the archived pytrends) or apply to the official free alpha API (rolling approval, quota-limited). HONEST FLAGS: pytrends is archived/fragile — don't build on it; the Apify 'free Google Trends' actor isn't actually free ($5/1k); Glimpse/ScrapingBee are paid and unnecessary; and TikTok/Snapchat (both major in Saudi) have NO comparable free engagement API — Snapchat is a real blind spot. Recommended wiring order for the 3 pilots: own-account Graph API (free, biggest signal) -> Google Trends via trendspyg (free, demand layer) -> Apify competitor pulls (cheap, already-funded) -> defer business_discovery App Review until pilots prove value. The genuinely free-now-and-Saudi-ready set covers own-account + demand-trends; everything that reads competitors at scale costs small Apify credits or needs Meta review — don't promise free competitor intelligence. Key repos/urls: https://github.com/flack0x/trendspyg (free trends lib, v0.6.1 2026-06-08), https://developers.google.com/search/apis/trends (official alpha, 2025-07-24), https://developers.facebook.com/docs/instagram-platform/reference/instagram-media/ , https://apify.com/apify/instagram-scraper , https://github.com/GeneralMills/pytrends (archived — avoid).

synthesis.google_brand_ai: Google built almost EXACTLY what OGZ is building — and that is good news, not bad. Pomelli (Google Labs + DeepMind, launched Oct 28 2025) runs OGZ's exact spine: give it a URL, it extracts a "Business DNA" profile (tone of voice, fonts, color palette, visual style), suggests campaign IDEAS from that DNA, then generates on-brand assets. That is OGZ's per-client-organs → ideas → captions loop, hand-built by Google. BUT Google has NOT shipped it for OGZ's market. As of March 2026 Pomelli expanded to 170+ countries (so it works in Saudi as a COUNTRY), yet it is STILL English-only (no Arabic) and STILL has NO public API (confirmed Google Labs help + LinkGo FAQ). So Arabic-native, Saudi-taste, autonomous Instagram remains a real gap Google has left open — that IS OGZ's moat. The ONLY Google piece OGZ can wire is the raw Gemini API (confirmed available in Saudi on ai.google.dev/gemini-api/docs/available-regions; strong Arabic, JSON-schema output, 1M context to hold a client corpus) — but with two honest flags: (1) MONEY — OpenAI is the funded key, Gemini's free quota is exhausted and billing isn't enabled, so don't switch engines just for this; (2) the durable asset is the PROMPT/architecture, not Google's brand. The right move: COPY Pomelli's structure (corpus/URL → structured Business-DNA JSON → ideas → captions) and Google Ads' "AI Brief / what-to-AVOID" negative-brief pattern, and implement them on the already-funded OpenAI key. Google Product Studio (free, image-only, no Arabic copy, video gated to non-Saudi countries) and Google Ads AI (locked inside paid ad campaigns, no standalone content API, ties output to Google ad inventory not Instagram) are NOT usable for the 3 IG pilots. Use Pomelli itself for free as a benchmark: sign up from KSA in English, run an albaik/jirish/myfitness site through it, screenshot its Business DNA + ideas, and let Mohamed taste-test Google's English ideas vs OGZ's Arabic ones.

wire_now_free.tool: Apify — Instagram scraper + Google Maps Reviews Scraper (already-owned paid Starter token)
wire_now_free.into_stage: RESEARCH (new stage 1, before strategy) — fattens thin organs with real market data
wire_now_free.changes: Scrape each pilot's own IG posts + top 3-5 Saudi competitors + Google Maps reviews. Reviews scraper is ~$0.25/1k results; IG posts via Apify. This is the cheapest, most direct fix for thin data like myfitness's 6 moments — turns a 6-moment skeleton into a real corpus of how THIS brand and its rivals actually post and what customers actually say. $0 extra beyond the existing token for pilot-size pulls.

wire_now_free.tool: Engagement-winner analysis pass (pure compute over the scraped data)
wire_now_free.into_stage: RESEARCH → DIAGNOSE — produces the per-client gap_report (Rule #11)
wire_now_free.changes: Rank the brand's own + competitors' posts by engagement, extract what wins for THIS brand (post types, hooks, posting cadence, hashtag patterns, top topics). Output is data-grounded 'what wins here' instead of a generic template — and the gaps it can't answer become the gap-questions OGZ asks the client. Free (compute only).

wire_now_free.tool: CAMeLBERT-DA Saudi-dialect sentiment (open, free, runs local on the Mac Mini)
wire_now_free.changes: Classify Saudi-dialect reviews and IG comments for sentiment + theme. Matters because generic/English sentiment mislabels Gulf Arabic — a 2026 PeerJ study fine-tuned this exact model for Saudi-dialect customer sentiment (peerj.com/articles/cs-3511/). $0/query, no API limits, Saudi-dialect-aware. Surfaces the brand's real strengths/complaints in the customers' own words to seed strategy + red-lines.

wire_now_free.tool: Arabic embeddings for the per-client RAG memory (open, free, local)
wire_now_free.into_stage: MEMORY layer feeding RESEARCH + caption generation
wire_now_free.changes: Embed the scraped corpus into the per-client RAG so the brains READ the brand's real material when writing, keeping per-client memory consistent (the stated requirement). $0/query, local. This is how you replicate Pomelli's 'Business DNA' for Arabic without Google's brand.

needs_account_or_money: Instagram Graph API insights (reach/engagement/saves/audience demographics) — FREE but needs the CLIENT'S CONSENT: the pilot's IG must be a Business/Creator account linked to a Facebook Page, plus an access token with instagram_manage_insights + pages_read_engagement. Without that, you only get public post counts via scraping, never the true reach/saves numbers. This is a per-client gate, not a money gate — stage it as a one-tap ask in the client onboarding.

Gemini API billing — would need billing enabled on the GCP project (free key is EXHAUSTED). Do NOT enable just for this; OpenAI is already funded and the structure (not Google's model) is the value. Keep Gemini only as a cheap second opinion IF billing is ever turned on.

Apify beyond pilot scale — the free tier is only ~$5/mo platform credits; OGZ already has a paid Starter token, so pilot-size pulls are covered, but a full client roster would consume credits (Google Maps base ~$4/1k, reviews ~$0.25/1k). Budget it per-client, not unlimited.
Pomelli itself — free to USE as a benchmark (sign up from KSA in English), but cannot be WIRED: no public API, English-only. Account is free; integration is impossible.

Official Google Trends API — in alpha with limited endpoints/quotas; pytrends was archived April 2025 and is now unreliable (breaks often, incomplete data). Treat Trends as a nice-to-have, not a dependency — don't build the research stage on it.
synthesis.read_data_better: Stop reading the client as a static profile and start reading the client as a CORPUS + its market. Concretely: (1) pull the brand's own IG posts, 3-5 Saudi competitors, and Google Maps reviews via the already-paid Apify token; (2) embed that corpus into a per-client Arabic RAG (Arabic-Triplet-Matryoshka-V2) so the brains read the brand's REAL words when they write — not a 6-line organ; (3) run Saudi-dialect sentiment (CAMeLBERT-DA) over reviews + comments to extract real strengths, complaints, and red-lines in the customers' own dialect; (4) where consent exists, layer the client's true reach/saves/audience from the IG Graph API on top. This is exactly Pomelli's 'Business DNA' move (read the brand FIRST, then generate on-brand) — but Arabic-native and deeper, built on the funded OpenAI key. It directly fixes the thin-organ problem: myfitness's 6 moments become a corpus of how the brand + its Tabuk-gym rivals actually post and what members actually praise/complain about.
synthesis.better_strategy: A good strategy is FALSIFIABLE and brand-specific; a generic one is a template with the brand name pasted in. Four things separate them: (1) It is built FROM the brand's own data — every claim ('post more behind-the-kitchen reels') traces to a real engagement-winner in THIS brand's or a direct competitor's scraped feed, not a best-practice listicle. (2) It names what to AVOID — borrow Google Ads' 'negative brief' pattern: per-client what-to-avoid (Saudi red-lines + this brand's kill_patterns) is captured explicitly, because a strategy that only says what to do is half a strategy. (3) Its gaps are honest — it surfaces what the brand's own data CANNOT tell us and turns THOSE into the client gap-questions (Rule #11: diagnose → show → wait), instead of pretending completeness. (4) It is testable in artifacts, not theory — it predicts which post types win and Mohamed rates real outputs (20/batch) against that prediction; his judgment feeds gold/kills and the next batch improves. Generic = unverifiable advice anyone could give any gym. Good = 'for myfitness specifically, grounded in these 40 scraped posts and these 120 Saudi-dialect reviews, do X, avoid Y, and here are the 3 gaps only you can answer.'
synthesis.recommended_first_move: Wire ONE thing this week: an Apify pull for a single pilot (start with myfitness — the thinnest, 6 moments) = its own IG posts + 3 Tabuk/Saudi gym competitors + its Google Maps reviews, run CAMeLBERT-DA sentiment over the reviews, and produce a one-page data-grounded gap_report. Show Mohamed THAT (diagnose → show → wait, Rule #11) before building any pipeline. It proves the research-before-strategy stage on real data, costs ~$0 on the owned token, needs no new keys, and turns the weakest pilot into the demo that the corpus approach beats the thin organ. Do NOT touch Gemini billing or build the full stage ahead of his nod.
synthesis.honest_caveats: 1) GitHub MCP returned 'Bad credentials' this session — I could not pull live repo stars/code; repo landscape was verified via web search instead, so treat specific repo recommendations as 'category exists and is open-source,' not 'audited.' 2) Pomelli's status moves fast — it was 4 countries at Oct 2025 launch, 170+ by March 2026; Arabic + an API are 'planned future' per coverage, so the moat could narrow — re-check ai.google.dev and Google Labs help quarterly. 3) IG insights are gated behind the CLIENT's consent (Business account + Facebook Page + token); without it you get scraped public counts only, never true reach/saves — don't promise audience demographics until consent lands. 4) Apify free tier is only ~$5/mo; the owned Starter token covers pilots but not a full roster — budget per-client. 5) pytrends is archived (April 2025) and unreliable, official Trends API is alpha — don't build on Trends. 6) Gemini free quota is exhausted and OpenAI is funded — switching the brain to Gemini for this would burn money for no durable gain; the PROMPT/architecture is the asset, per money-discipline. 7) Open Arabic sentiment models are strong on dialect but still need a small Saudi-calibration check before any number reaches Mohamed (Rule #9: verify the number before you say it).
